name: Sourdough French Bread#3
recipeIngredient: 3 cups All-purpose flour (I use - unbleached Gold Medal)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix the dry yeast with body-heat water (about 1/4 to 1/2 cup).
2. Add 2 cups of the flour, the starter, salt, and sugar to the yeast mixture and stir for about 10 minutes until smooth.
3. Cover with a damp towel and let rise for at least 1 1/2 hours.
4. Stir in one cup of flour or enough to make the dough rigid.
5. Knead dough on a well-floured bread board for 10-15 minutes.
6. Roll into a long loaf or form into a round ball, depending on your preference.
7. Place on a baking sheet sprinkled with corn meal.
8. Cover with a damp towel and let rise for at least 1 1/2 hours (this allows you to enjoy other activities).
9. In the oven bottom, place a pan filled with boiling water (the secret weapon).
10. Mix half a tablespoon of cornstarch in about 1/4 cup of hot water.
11. Make several diagonal slices in the top of the loaf to prevent it from breaking up when it fills with steam; brush the loaf with the cornstarch mixture.
12. Place the loaf (on the bread pan sprinkled with corn meal) in the oven above the water pan and bake at 400 degrees for 10-12 minutes.
13. Remove from the oven, and brush again with the cornstarch mixture.
14. Remove the water pan from the oven and refill it with cold water.
15. Place the bread and water pan back in the oven (water pan below the bread) and bake for about 25 minutes.
